steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage repeatedly names natural elements (wild rice, lizard, wild goose, cranes, twilight, sedges) without advancing a narrative or argument. Each line or pair of lines isolates a distinct image of the marshland.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice B aligns with the text’s pattern of presenting discrete natural images to build the setting.

A splits animals/plants, which the text never organizes in such rigid categories; C and D introduce human references or arguments absent in the poem.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
